class Solution:
    def minimumCost(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        from collections import Counter

        def is_palindrome(x):
            return str(x) == str(x)[::-1]

        # Generate all palindromes up to 10^9
        palindromes = set()
        for i in range(1, 100000):  # Covers up to 10^5 digits
            s = str(i)
            palindromes.add(int(s + s[::-1]))
            palindromes.add(int(s + s[::-1][1:]))  # For odd length palindromes

        # Count frequency of each number in nums
        freq = Counter(nums)

        # Find the median (or median-like value) of nums
        sorted_nums = sorted(nums)
        n = len(sorted_nums)
        median = sorted_nums[n // 2]

        # Find the closest palindrome to the median
        min_cost = float('inf')
        for p in palindromes:
            if abs(p - median) < abs(p - median):
                continue
            cost = 0
            for num in nums:
                cost += abs(num - p)
            min_cost = min(min_cost, cost)

        return min_cost
